recipes.name: Code Map
recipes.summary: Adds a lightweight code-map discipline to agent development work.
recipes.description: Prompts agents to inspect or update a compact project code map before implementation, then reports whether the map was used through a managed prompt-module mutation set.

skills.summary: Keeps a concise project code map aligned with implementation work.

agents.summary: Applies the code-map discipline during coding and verification tasks.

scenarios.name: Create or Refresh Code Map
scenarios.summary: Create or refresh a compact project code map before implementation.
scenarios.description: Use this when a task touches multiple code areas or when the repository lacks a current map of entry points, runtime surfaces, and verification commands.
use_when: A coding task spans multiple modules or ownership boundaries.
A new agent needs a quick map of the project's runtime surfaces.
A change depends on understanding entry points and verification commands.
avoid_when: The task is a one-line change with an obvious local owner.
The repository already has a current code map for the touched area.

prompt_modules.summary: Recipe-owned policy module for code-map discipline.

prompt_mutation_sets.summary: Registers the code-map policy module in the managed prompt graph.

recipes.name: LLM Wiki
recipes.summary: Adds an agent-maintained project wiki with raw-source ingestion, query routing, and stale-input guardrails.
recipes.description: Prompts agents to maintain a project-local llm-wiki namespace with an input inbox, immutable raw archive, structured wiki pages, index-based retrieval, and an explicit assimilation stop rule before unrelated task work.

skills.summary: Maintains a project-local LLM wiki from raw incoming sources.

agents.summary: Owns wiki initialization, source assimilation, index maintenance, and query retrieval discipline.

scenarios.name: Initialize or Assimilate LLM Wiki
scenarios.summary: Create the llm-wiki structure or assimilate incoming source files before project work continues.
scenarios.description: Use this when a repository needs an agent-maintained wiki, when llm-wiki/_input contains unprocessed files, or when project knowledge must be structured before answering or implementation.
use_when: The project has no llm-wiki namespace yet.
llm-wiki/_input or the configured input directory contains unprocessed files.
A request depends on project knowledge that should be routed through llm-wiki/index.md.
avoid_when: The task is unrelated and the configured input directory is empty.
The repository deliberately does not want a tracked wiki namespace.

prompt_modules.summary: Recipe-owned policy module for LLM wiki source assimilation and retrieval discipline.

prompt_mutation_sets.summary: Requires the LLM wiki policy module to be present when the recipe is active.
